q: Millennial couple — best approach?
o: Standard scripted pitch with emphasis on warranty length and brand heritage
Lead with lowest price point, discount offers, and clearance models only
Focus exclusively on health certifications, foam density specs, and lab results
Lifestyle benefits, transparency, and real reviews + technology story

o: Lowest price tag available, clearance models, and basic options
Trendiest new model with social media buzz and modern aesthetics
Fastest delivery timeline, same-day pickup, and quick decisions
Health benefits, ease of use, base demo, and long-term value

o: Premium luxury model with advanced features and adjustable base included
Most expensive option with full accessory bundle and white-glove setup
Basic mattress only with no protector — students don't need extras
Durable, affordable, easy setup — and always include a protector

o: 'What's your total budget range so I can narrow down the right price tier for you?'
'Which brand names have you been looking at or heard the most about online?'
'How soon do you need delivery — are you on a tight timeline for this purchase?'
'Where specifically — lower, upper, or both? What position do you sleep in?'

q: Hot sleeper profiling — what to emphasize?
o: Plush feel layers, soft quilted tops, and thick comfort padding for coziness
Total coil count, gauge thickness, and innerspring construction specifications
Pillow height options, fill material choices, and pillow firmness preferences
Cooling technology, breathable covers, and airflow-optimized construction

q: Couple with different firmness preferences?
o: Default to the softer option since it pleases more sleepers
Go with the firmer mattress and add a topper to adjust feel
Tell them to compromise and settle on a universal medium feel
Split king adjustable base — each side fully customizable

q: Guest who researched everything online — approach?

Challenge their knowledge head-on by citing technical specs and brand comparisons
Simplify everything down to basic options and skip the technical conversation entirely

o: Luxury premium mattress only — high guest ratings require top-tier products
Cheapest available option to minimize cost since guests won't notice the difference
Standard mattress without a protector — frequent sheet changes are enough protection
Durable mid-range with heavy-duty waterproof protector — durability is priority

q: Guest with fibromyalgia — what matters most?
o: Firmness level only — extra-firm support is the primary concern for chronic pain
Brand name reputation and recognition — trusted names provide the most confidence
Lowest price point only — condition management shouldn't require premium spending
Pressure relief and temperature regulation — pain sensitivity requires extra care

q: Night shift worker — what do they need?
o: Standard mattress with typical features — sleep schedule doesn't change needs
Cheapest option available since they spend fewer consecutive hours sleeping anyway
Luxury top-tier mattress only — nothing else will work for irregular schedules
Blackout-compatible setup, temperature regulation, maximum comfort for daytime sleep

q: First-time mattress buyer — what do they need most?
o: Show the most expensive option first — premium products make the best first purchase
Dive into the most technical explanation of coil systems, foam layers, and materials
Let them browse independently — first-time buyers prefer no guidance or pressure
Education first — walk them through types, firmness, and sleep position basics

q: Guest trading up from a $400 mattress — approach?

Immediately jump to the most expensive model to maximize the upgrade experience
Ignore their mattress history entirely and treat them as a brand-new buyer

Only mattress size matters — upgrade to a larger bed for more room during pregnancy
Standard recommendation works fine — use same approach as any other guest profile

No specific recommendation is possible — sleep apnea is a medical issue only doctors handle

Go with the firmest option available — older adults always need maximum firm support

High-density foam, reinforced coils, strong edge support, and firm to medium-firm feel

o: Avoid soft mattresses — lighter guests sink too deep and lose proper alignment
Avoid any hybrid model — the coil system is unnecessary for lighter body types
Avoid all-foam mattresses — lighter sleepers need coils for adequate responsiveness
Very firm mattresses — lighter guests don't compress enough to activate support

Supportive, durable, waterproof protector essential, age-appropriate size

No recommendation possible — stomach sleeping pain needs medical care

Only useful for price negotiation tactics — doesn't affect product recommendation quality

q: What does 'lifestyle selling' mean in mattress retail?
